base_case: Bear/corrective (30–60 day horizon) — confirmed institutional-distribution correction from the June 2 ATH remains intact and not yet bottomed. Golden Cross regime technically holds (50d > 200d, price +7.2% above 50-day), but the path of least resistance is lower toward the 23.6% Fib (7,330) and primary re-entry at the 38.2% Fib (7,151), with maximum-conviction add at the 61.8% Fib/SMA-50 convergence near 6,860.
trend_regime: Golden Cross intact: 50-day above 200-day, price above both. +7.2% premium to 50-day SMA (compressed 3.4% from +10.6% on June 2) and +11.8% premium to 200-day SMA. VIX at the 20 threshold makes moving averages explicitly less reliable.
valuation_bucket: 20–22x Forward P/E (21.17x) — cautious dip-buying posture; buy only on Fib + SMA + sentiment confluence. ERP improved to +0.223% but still historically thin.

signals.bollinger_position: within band — corrective pullback from upper-band pierce on June 2

what_changed_today: Nominal +0.30% green close (7,405.73) masks a 2nd consecutive distribution day: opened near high (7,466), sold off all day, closed in bottom 15% of range — same structure as June 5.
McClellan breadth (Stock Price Breadth) downgraded from Fear to EXTREME FEAR (~990, below 1,000) — worst breadth since the May correction; internals deteriorating faster than price.
Put/Call ratio rose from ~0.60 to ~0.68 — first constructive (contrarian) signal in 10 sessions; bears beginning to hedge, often appears 2–5 sessions before a local bottom.
F&G fell to 40 (Fear) from 42 — now −21 points from the May 26 peak of 61 over 9 sessions, the most rapid sustained collapse since October 2025.
ERP improved to +0.223% (from +0.097% cycle low on June 2) as Forward P/E compressed to 21.17x; valuation trend improving but still thin.
Monte Carlo primary first-hit improved marginally to 51.2% upside / 48.5% downside (from 47.8% on June 5) due to lower starting price — still decisively below the 65% action threshold.
6-month chart confirms SMA-50 now at ~6,863, within 3 points of the 61.8% Fib (6,860) — dual technical support convergence is the maximum-conviction re-entry zone.
23.6% Fib (7,330) now only 76 pts / −1.0% away — one bad open from a test; P(23.6% Fib in 60d) = 83.1%.
narrative_summary: The June 2 ATH correction remains intact and has not yet bottomed: today's nominal +0.30% green close hides a second consecutive open-high-to-close-near-low distribution day, and the McClellan breadth index was downgraded to Extreme Fear as internals deteriorate faster than price. With Wave 1 (7,696) never executed, the position holds full SCHK exposure into the correction; the Monte Carlo edge (51.2% upside-first) stays below the 65% threshold and VIX sits at the 20 risk flag, so the disciplined stance is hold and monitor — no trim, no re-entry yet. The single constructive development is the Put/Call ratio's first uptick in 10 sessions (~0.60 → ~0.68), historically an early bottoming watch signal, but breadth capitulation without the VIX spike (25–30) seen at prior cycle bottoms means this is a watch signal, not yet an action signal. Immediate tripwire is the 23.6% Fib at 7,330 (−1.0%): an intraday print below warrants the first 25% re-entry tranche, with the 38.2% Fib at 7,151 as the primary re-entry zone.
open_questions: Does the 23.6% Fib at 7,330 hold as support this week, or does a bad open break it toward 7,151?
Will the VIX spike to the 25–30 capitulation range that marked every prior cycle bottom (April, October 2025), or will this correction be shallower?
Is the Put/Call uptick to ~0.68 the start of genuine contrarian hedging that precedes a local bottom, or a one-session blip?
Will junk-bond spreads (5th consecutive day at 1.45%+ Extreme Fear) compress, or resolve via a VIX spike to meet credit stress?
Does McClellan breadth at Extreme Fear stabilize/uptick (a re-entry checklist item) or continue deteriorating?

decision_matrix.valuation: Forward P/E 21.17x (improving from 21.75x); ERP +0.223% — caution zone, still historically thin. Cautious dip-buying posture.
decision_matrix.technicals: Golden Cross intact but price +7.2% above 50-day and compressing; 2nd consecutive distribution day; RSI ~35 declining toward oversold; 23.6% Fib (7,330) only 76 pts away.
decision_matrix.sentiment: F&G 40 (Fear, −21 from May 26 peak); McClellan breadth downgraded to Extreme Fear; junk spreads Extreme Fear 5th day; Put/Call ~0.68 first constructive uptick; VIX ~20 elevated flag.
decision_matrix.risk: Elevated — VIX at the 20 risk threshold (moving averages less reliable, forced-selling risk rises); breadth at Extreme Fear but no capitulation VIX spike yet.

confirming_evidence: Golden Cross regime technically intact: 50d > 200d, price above both (+7.2% / +11.8%).
Second consecutive open-high-to-close-near-low distribution day confirms the corrective structure from the June 2 ATH.
McClellan breadth downgraded to Extreme Fear (~990) — worst since May correction; breadth erosion confirms institutional distribution.
F&G collapsed to 40 (Fear), −21 in 9 sessions; Safe Haven and Stock Price Strength in Fear; junk spreads Extreme Fear 5th straight day.
Monte Carlo: P(ATH 7,621 first vs 23.6% Fib 7,330) only 34.9%/65.1% — model favors 23.6% Fib touched first; P(23.6% Fib in 60d) 83.1%.
RSI fallen from ~80 (June 2) to ~35 and still declining; momentum decisively rolled over.
Wave 1 distance now +290 pts (+3.91%) — melt-up unlikely near-term.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Put/Call ratio rose from ~0.60 to ~0.68 — first constructive hedging signal in 10 sessions, historically appears 2–5 sessions before a local bottom (contrarian buy precursor).
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: McClellan breadth simultaneously downgraded to Extreme Fear (~990) and Stock Price Strength weakening — internals deteriorating, no confirmed bottom yet.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Sentiment must be confirmed by technical levels; a single contrarian uptick is a watch signal, not an action signal. Mixed data = hold and monitor.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Golden Cross intact, price still +7.2% above 50-day, Market Momentum in Greed (SPX above 125-day MA), long-term P(Wave 1 in 60d) 68.3%.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Two consecutive distribution days, breadth Extreme Fear, F&G in Fear, junk Extreme Fear 5th day — every internal layer flipped bearish while price holds in upper half of corrective zone.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: VIX > 20 makes moving averages less reliable; do not lean on intact SMA regime alone. Hold core, await Fib support definition.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Forward P/E compressed to 21.17x and ERP improved to +0.223% — valuation trend moving in the right direction for dip-buyers.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP still historically thin; needs pullback to 7,151 (~0.55% ERP) or 7,005 (~0.70% ERP) to represent compelling value — current level not yet a buy.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: 20–22x Forward P/E = cautious; buy only on Fib + SMA + sentiment confluence, which is not yet present.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Primary first-hit improved to 51.2% upside (Wave 1) vs 48.5% downside; P(7,500 in 60d) 86.2% suggests near-term recovery likely at some point.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: P(ATH 7,621 first vs 23.6% Fib 7,330) is only 34.9%/65.1% and P(23.6% Fib in 60d) 83.1% — model strongly favors a downside test first; all primary edges remain below the 65% action threshold.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Monte Carlo < 65% = insufficient edge; flag monitor-only, do not recommend action.

primary_tension: A marginally improving setup — first Put/Call uptick in 10 sessions, ERP rising to +0.223%, lower Forward P/E, and a Monte Carlo edge nudging back toward 50/50 — versus a still-deepening institutional distribution: a 2nd consecutive close-near-low distribution day, McClellan breadth downgraded to Extreme Fear, junk spreads at April-shock levels for a 5th session, F&G in Fear, and a model that still favors the 23.6% Fib being touched first (65.1%). With every primary edge below 65% and VIX at the 20 risk flag, the resolution is capital preservation: hold full core, define re-entry by Fib support, and wait for the VIX-spike/breadth-capitulation bottom signature rather than forcing a trade.

monte_carlo.median_days: Not explicitly stated; 23.6% Fib (7,330) flagged as easily reached as soon as Tuesday given it is only −1.0% / 76 pts away.
